author: @fredo226
text: Hello , is there anyway I can get something custom made with you? I would love to reach out.

author: @nico-bf1kr
text:
Could you show us how to make a foam? A bio-foam!
A solid one like in surfboard and a soft one like acoustic foam.
Foams are classified as a composite material. But here the reinforcement fibers (flax fiber per exemple) are replaced by air / gaz bubbles.
I guess a bio-plastic (that cure fast?) underpressure with something that produce CO2 inside the same container may be a method...
I would love to see a surfboard that desintegrate after a season! Could be even an edible one!

author: @williamgrimberg2510
text: Robert , I was involved in my dad’s business of custom molding for ten years where we did injection molding and transfer and compression molding . The business itself was in business for 48 years in Southern California servicing the air a space, medical and commercial industries. I  worked with many types of fiber filled polymers where we would pre dry the material in industrial ovens and microwave ovens before molding in either heated compression or transfer molds .  I am interested to know more about your experience with the carbonized hemp fibers that you have been working on and would like to contact you to maybe ask a few questions.

replies.author: @havanaday9981
replies.text: May I ask, will heat keep hemp fiber in it’s form after being pressed. Another individual uses 6”x6”x6’ cube molds for his logs to retain their shape while being baked. I’m trying to bypass that step. Any idea?

author: @lindavennberg
text:
Hello Robert! 
Thank you for this video! 
Can you replace casein with a plant based alternative to make it a "vegan" product?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: basically the casein is just a ready source of protein as around 50% of milk production just goes down the drain - almost literally -it amazed me - but anyway, the protein is the real plastic here - so yes it can be replaced - i am thinking soya?

I noticed a lot of you tube DIY peeps show off their gear and then that's it...I'm going the other way lol Right now I have made a Silver Collodial /Graphene Oxide spray that research indicates kills the Corona virus and pretty well all cotties lol (topical app)

author: @sarchlalaith8836
text: @Robert Murray-Smith on that's brilliant! I'm sold... But, hard as stone you say? Are we talking stopping bullets like kevlar/aramid... Because I'm 100% interested in seeing how this performs as armour in stopping bullets.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I did a version of this with a graphene additive and it did indeed stop bullets - I took it to a firing range and shot a 45mm handgun and AK47 at it

author: @danp1224
text: Hi Robert. If you add borax and urea to your casein it will have water resistance. Hope it helps. You have given me so much info. It’s been around for 100 of years.

author: @JamesWilson-pq9qp
text: Edison used so many different carbons before the days of our petroleum plastics, Henry Ford was always so fascinated with his research,  they would talk for hours and worked together on environmental issues.  It's so good to see the research continuing, thanks Rob.  ... You need a Henry!  ...  Imagine having to depend only on bakelite.    Bakelite Museum  in    Williton, Somerset, England.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have a 'Henry' mate - his name is Steve Mills - an awesome guy

replies.author: @JamesWilson-pq9qp
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  I though so ... Sent thanks to Steve! ... P.S.  Blakelite stopped Edison's research - competition, frustration and papers were lost.

author: @4gauge10
text:
Excellent video Robert,very"impressed".
Now,I'm wondering about one thing.
Has this been tested with mice?.
This question may seem strange to you,but the reason why I'm asking this is because hemp being plant based,tends to draw rodents.
In the automotive industry,their is a serious problem with mice eating the wiring in cars.The wiring now days uses soy based coating on the wires,being also plant based,again,draws rodents and has caused many major electrical fires.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's an awesome question mate and I understand where you are coming from - but no - it hasn't been tested - never thought of it and no mice - still very good point - I am going to cover some with chocolate and see if Luke eats it lol

replies.author: @RWBHere
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering If he's a normal teenager (is there such a thing?) I wouldn't put it past him trying to eat it. Let's hope that he doesn't try to smoke that hemp!
